findings.title: Fixed 6-second spinner timeout not tied to actual provider completion
findings.description: The spinner is stopped via a hardcoded setTimeout of 6000ms regardless of whether all providers have actually resolved. If any provider (e.g. Audnexus doing 5 serial Audnexus fetches after the Audible catalog lookup) takes longer than 6s, results arrive after the spinner stops — UI looks done but is still receiving data. Conversely if all providers finish in 1s, spinner runs an extra 5s unnecessarily. There is no mechanism to count resolved providers and stop early.
findings.recommendation: Track pending provider count. Decrement on each deliver() call; set isSearching(false) when count reaches 0. Fall back to the 6s timeout only as a safety net.

findings.title: CoverSearchGrid uses array index as React key — selection breaks on progressive result appends
findings.description: Results are keyed by array index (`key={index}`) and selection is tracked by index. When new provider results are appended to the searchResults array, existing items shift indexes if results from a later provider are prepended, but more critically the selectedIndex saved for result[3] now refers to a different result after more items arrive. This causes the visual checkmark to jump to the wrong card.
findings.recommendation: Use a stable key derived from provider+asin/isbn/title combo. Track selected result by reference (or stable composite key), not by index.

findings.title: AudnexusService calls Audible API directly from the browser — always fails due to CORS
findings.description: The function calls `https://api.audible.com/1.0/catalog/products` directly in the browser. The comment acknowledges CORS will block it but does not fall back to the proxy. The server proxy at `/api/audible/proxy` is defined in server/index.ts but is never used by AudnexusService. As a result, the Audible catalog search always silently fails and Audnexus results are only available when the caller already provides an ASIN.
findings.recommendation: In searchAudibleCatalog, call `/api/audible/proxy?title=...&author=...` instead of the Audible URL directly.

findings.title: MetadataSearchResult interface duplicated in AudnexusService.ts and CoverSearchService.ts
findings.description: Both files define an identical MetadataSearchResult interface. AudnexusService exports its own copy; CoverSearchService exports the canonical one. If the two ever diverge (e.g. a new provider field is added to one but not the other) type errors will not surface because each file uses its own local interface. The services also import from each other indirectly through the aggregator, creating potential for subtle type mismatches.
findings.recommendation: Remove the duplicate interface from AudnexusService.ts and import MetadataSearchResult from CoverSearchService.ts (or a shared types file).

findings.title: handleSelectResult closes over stale form state — auto-fill conditions may incorrectly skip fields
findings.description: handleSelectResult's useCallback dependency array includes author, description, seriesName, seriesSequence, isbn, asin, narrator, genre. If the user edits a field and then clicks a result card before React re-renders the memo (which should be rare but possible in rapid interactions), the auto-fill check `!author.trim()` evaluates against stale state and may skip filling a field that should have been filled, or fill one that shouldn't be.
findings.recommendation: This is acceptable for the common case, but if stricter correctness is needed, use functional state reads (useRef for latest values) or read state directly inside the callback. At minimum, document the known limitation.

findings.title: Server proxy does not validate or sanitize query parameters before forwarding
findings.description: The proxy reads title, author, num_results, and response_groups directly from req.query and passes them into the URL without validation. An attacker can supply an arbitrary response_groups string or override num_results to a very large value (e.g. 500), increasing load on Audible and potentially the server response size. The num_results parameter is also forwarded as-is from the user, allowing arbitrary values.
findings.recommendation: Whitelist allowed response_groups values and clamp num_results to a safe maximum (e.g. 10).

findings.title: normalizeAndDedup deduplicates within a single provider batch but not across providers
findings.description: normalizeAndDedup is called per-provider result set with a fresh seenIsbn/seenAsin set each time. Cross-provider duplicates (e.g. same ISBN from both Google Books and Open Library) are never removed from searchResults. The UI will show duplicate cards for the same book from multiple providers.
findings.recommendation: Maintain a shared dedup set in the searchCovers closure and pass or check it across provider deliveries, or deduplicate in the consumer (setSearchResults in the editor) before rendering.

findings.title: toJpeg creates an object URL that is revoked before img.onload fires in some browsers
findings.description: The objectUrl is revoked synchronously after `img.src = objectUrl` and the `await new Promise` but BEFORE img.onload resolves — actually reading the code more carefully, the revoke happens AFTER the await resolves. This is correct. However if img.onerror fires (e.g. network image that cannot be decoded), URL.revokeObjectURL is still called and the error is propagated correctly. No bug here — confidence downgraded. Flagged as low-risk observation.
findings.recommendation: No change needed; the URL lifecycle is correct.

findings.title: Open Library adapter silently drops results that have description/subjects but no coverUrl
findings.description: The condition `if (!result.coverUrl && !result.description && !result.subjects) return []` means a result with only a coverUrl OR only description+subjects passes through. However a result that has a description and subjects but no coverUrl will still be included — which is intentional. But a result with ONLY coverUrl and no description/subjects is also included, which means metadata-only Open Library results (no cover) are discarded silently. This may be intentional but is worth verifying against the intent.
findings.recommendation: Verify intent: should Open Library results without a cover but with rich metadata (description, genres) still be surfaced? If yes, the condition is fine. Document explicitly.

findings.title: Abort signal passed to searchCovers is checked only at delivery time, not inside provider fetches
findings.description: The AbortSignal is checked in `deliver()` before calling onResults, but it is NOT passed to the underlying fetchWithTimeout calls in GoogleBooksService, ITunesSearchService, or AudnexusService. Aborting the controller cancels result delivery but does not cancel in-flight HTTP requests — network resources are wasted and responses are processed up to the point of deliver().
findings.recommendation: Thread the AbortSignal into each service's fetch call so network requests are actually cancelled on abort.

residual_risks.risk: Audnexus never returns results in browser without the proxy being called — feature is silently broken for ASIN-less audiobooks
Progressive result streaming + index-based selection creates incorrect selection state whenever results arrive after initial render
Cross-provider deduplication absent — users see same book listed 2-3 times
6s hardcoded spinner creates misleading 'done' signal when slow providers are still in flight

testing_gaps.gap: No test covers the CORS failure path in searchAudibleCatalog and the proxy fallback (which doesn't exist yet)
No test verifies selectedIndex stability when new results are appended mid-selection
No test verifies cross-provider deduplication behaviour
No test for handleSelectResult auto-fill with pre-populated fields — verifies fields are not overwritten
No test for server proxy parameter injection / num_results clamping
AudnexusService unit tests (listed as untracked) should include the CORS bypass path via proxy
